rationale: In the hypothesis-development section for H2, the citation is tied to a specific claim that CMOs have formal marketing training and advanced analytical expertise, rather than being a generic background mention. The citing paper uses that claim to support its mechanism that CMO presence can mitigate governance hazards and weaken the shift toward acquisitions. This role is part of the core conceptual framing of the moderator, even though the citing study does not reuse the cited paper's data or model.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Homburg et al. (2014) as prior evidence about CMO human capital and expertise. That evidence helps justify the theorized moderating role of CMO presence in the strategic emphasis-governance mode choice relationship.
